page: Microsoft Azure E16-8ds_v6 is an Edsv6-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8573C processor containing 8 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, the processor features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, and includes 945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2154.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 31.38 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is moderate, yielding a stress-ng multi-core score of 7989.66 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8272.0. Memory bandwidth tests show a cached small-block read speed of 621.15 GB/sec, which drops to 64.02 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. In database and key-value operations, the server achieves 11.88 million operations per second on Redis SET benchmarks and a PassMark database operations score of 4990.18.

The hardware configuration presents distinct performance tradeoffs. While the processor delivers strong text generation speeds of 276.83 tokens/sec for small 135M LLM models, performance degrades on larger models, dropping to a weak rating of 8.24 tokens/sec for medium 7B models and 6.74 tokens/sec for large 70B model prompt processing. Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local storage capacity, this instance is configured for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM relative to compute resources.
description: The Microsoft Azure E16-8ds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv6 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage. The architecture supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2154.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 31.38 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier, and LLM inference performance drops significantly on larger models, such as a weak 8.24 tokens/sec for 7B models. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per core, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and caching applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8ds_v6 is an Edsv6 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8ds_v6 is an Edsv6 virtual machine with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM-to-vCPU density, dedicated Intel Xeon processing, and integrated local storage for database workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C vCPUs operating at 4.2 GHz
128.0 GB of RAM providing a high 16:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
Strong single-core performance but limited large-scale LLM inference capabilities
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
